vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing

parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

requirements: Structural integrity under wing spar loads
Cooling airflow to motor pylon area
Equipment bay door functionality
Avionics and sensor housing
Service access for maintenance

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structures: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Interfaces: spar_socket, main_spar, anti_rotation_key, rib, carry_through, frame
Datum visibility: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Clearance: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Interface count: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Standard fit vocabulary: Fits are named with standard intent
Standard hardware dimensioning: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
Spar datum access: Spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable
Mission payload non-weapon boundary: Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module
CG battery service: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal
Load path continuity: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure

verify.rule: All required evidence_features are present.
Mass estimate is within acceptable range.
Each part_tree entry corresponds to a geometry_op.
Interfaces are reciprocal and consistent between subsystems.
Critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment of anti-rotation key due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances and conduct post-manufacture alignment checks.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for battery bay harness routing.
risk.mit: Review harness routing paths and ensure sufficient clearance is maintained.

risk.desc: Possibility of structural integrity issues if load path continuity is compromised.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough load path analysis and verification tests.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fit due to dimensional variations in standard hardware.
risk.mit: Use standardized hardware dimensions and conduct fit checks during assembly.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment at wing root interface due to rotational DOF.
risk.mit: Implement additional alignment features and conduct pre-assembly checks.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

risk.mit: Increase size or number of thermal dissipation fins.

risk.desc: Cable strain relief may not be sufficient if bend radius is too tight.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have proper strain relief and follow recommended bend radius.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels
physics_paths: Load path: Control horn to MG90S or DS215MG servo
Flow path: Pushrod channel for linkage
Thermal path: Heat dissipation from servo to wing surface
Motion path: Control horn movement translates to elevon deflection
Pressure path: Aerodynamic pressure on wing surface
integration_opportunities: Servo_mounting_flange can be optimized for weight reduction while maintaining strength.
Control_horn_socket can be designed for easy replacement of control horns.
Pushrod_entry_port can be engineered for minimal friction and wear resistance.
Access_cover can be made quick-release for maintenance.
Thermal_dissipation_fins can be adjusted for optimal heat transfer.
verification_focus: Mass verification: Ensure total mass does not exceed 100g.
Strength verification: Test servo mounting flange under maximum expected loads.
Seal verification: Check access cover seals properly against environmental ingress.
